findings.desc: Fee denominator uses 10000 instead of 1000, overcharging users ~10x the input amount on every swapExactOutput call.
Missing maxInputAmount slippage parameter lets users get drained via unbounded inputAmount during price movement or sandwich attacks.
The every-10-swaps free 1e18 token incentive drains reserves and breaks the x*y=k invariant, allowing eventual pool depletion.

The minimumLiquidityTokensToMint/liquidityTokensToMint check and parameter ordering bug (LiquidityAdded emits poolTokens and weth swapped) misvalue deposits, with initial-deposit path ignoring poolToken slippage protection.

proposer.raw:
HIGH | TSwapPool.getInputAmountBasedOnOutput | Fee denominator uses 10000 instead of 1000, overcharging users ~10x the input amount on every swapExactOutput call.
HIGH | TSwapPool.swapExactOutput | Missing maxInputAmount slippage parameter lets users get drained via unbounded inputAmount during price movement or sandwich attacks.
HIGH | TSwapPool._swap | The every-10-swaps free 1e18 token incentive drains reserves and breaks the x*y=k invariant, allowing eventual pool depletion.
HIGH | TSwapPool.deposit | The minimumLiquidityTokensToMint/liquidityTokensToMint check and parameter ordering bug (LiquidityAdded emits poolTokens and weth swapped) misvalue deposits, with initial-deposit path ignoring poolToken slippage protection.

results.finding: a swap can break the x*y=k constant-product invariant (fee / extra-token bug).
